info.description: REST API for Cosmos Cloud server management
info.title: Cosmos Server API

get.description: Returns the server log file as a plain text attachment
get.summary: Download server logs

get.description: Returns memory usage details for internal caches and buffers
get.summary: Get internal memory status
List or create monitoring alerts
Get, update, or delete a monitoring alert by name

get.description: Returns all configured API tokens (without the actual token hashes)

post.description: Generates a new API token with the specified permissions and returns the raw token (shown only once)

delete.description: Removes an API token by name

put.description: Updates an existing API token's description, permissions, IP whitelist, or constellation restriction

post.description: Creates a backup config and initializes the repository if needed. Requires premium licence and non-container mode.

get.description: Returns a single backup configuration from the server config.

Get repository statistics (size, file count, etc.)

get.description: Returns whether the server has email sending capabilities configured
get.summary: Check if email sending is enabled

argsEscaped.description: True if command is already escaped (meaning treat as a command line) (Windows specific).

attachStdin.description: Attach the standard input, makes possible user interaction

cmd.description: Command to run when starting the container

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint to run when starting the container

env.description: List of environment variable to set in the container

healthcheck.description: Healthcheck describes how to check the container is healthy

image.description: Name of the image as it was passed by the operator (e.g. could be symbolic)

labels.description: List of labels set to this container

macAddress.description:
Mac Address of the container.

Deprecated: this field is deprecated since API v1.44. Use EndpointSettings.MacAddress instead.

onBuild.description: ONBUILD metadata that were defined on the image Dockerfile

shell.description: Shell for shell-form of RUN, CMD, ENTRYPOINT

stdinOnce.description: If true, close stdin after the 1 attached client disconnects.

stopTimeout.description: Timeout (in seconds) to stop a container

tty.description: Attach standard streams to a tty, including stdin if it is not closed.

user.description: User that will run the command(s) inside the container, also support user:group

volumes.description: List of volumes (mounts) used for the container

workingDir.description: Current directory (PWD) in the command will be launched

interval.description: Zero means to inherit. Durations are expressed as integer nanoseconds.

retries.description:
Retries is the number of consecutive failures needed to consider a container as unhealthy.
Zero means inherit.

startInterval.description: The interval to attempt healthchecks at during the start period

startPeriod.description: The start period for the container to initialize before the retries starts to count down.

test.description:
Test is the test to perform to check that the container is healthy.
An empty slice means to inherit the default.
The options are:
{} : inherit healthcheck
{"NONE"} : disable healthcheck
{"CMD", args...} : exec arguments directly
{"CMD-SHELL", command} : run command with system's default shell

timeout.description: Timeout is the time to wait before considering the check to have hung.

interactive.description: we make this a int so that we can ignore 0

lastTagTime.description: LastTagTime is the date and time at which the image was last tagged.

readOnlyForceRecursive.description: ReadOnlyForceRecursive raises an error if the mount cannot be made recursively read-only.

readOnlyNonRecursive.description:
ReadOnlyNonRecursive makes the mount non-recursively read-only, but still leaves the mount recursive
(unless NonRecursive is set to true in conjunction).

source.description:
Source specifies the name of the mount. Depending on mount type, this
may be a volume name or a host path, or even ignored.
Source is not supported for tmpfs (must be an empty value)

mode.description: Mode of the tmpfs upon creation

sizeBytes.description:
Size sets the size of the tmpfs, in bytes.

This will be converted to an operating system specific value
depending on the host. For example, on linux, it will be converted to
use a 'k', 'm' or 'g' syntax. BSD, though not widely supported with
docker, uses a straight byte value.

Percentages are not supported.

HostIp.description: HostIP is the host IP Address

hostPort.description: HostPort is the host port number

x-enum-comments.ModeCharDevice: c: Unix character device, when ModeDevice is set
x-enum-comments.ModeIrregular: ?: non-regular file; nothing else is known about this file

x-enum-descriptions: c: Unix character device, when ModeDevice is set
?: non-regular file; nothing else is known about this file

Data.description:
Low-level storage metadata, provided as key/value pairs.

This information is driver-specific, and depends on the storage-driver
in use, and should be used for informational purposes only.

Required: true

Name.description:
Name of the storage driver.
Required: true

Id.description:
ID is the content-addressable ID of an image.

This identifier is a content-addressable digest calculated from the
image's configuration (which includes the digests of layers used by
the image).

Note that this digest differs from the `RepoDigests` below, which
holds digests of image manifests that reference the image.

VirtualSize.description:
VirtualSize is the total size of the image including all layers it is
composed of.

Deprecated: this field is omitted in API v1.44, but kept for backward compatibility. Use Size instead.

architecture.description: Architecture is the hardware CPU architecture that the image runs on.

author.description:
Author is the name of the author that was specified when committing the
image, or as specified through MAINTAINER (deprecated) in the Dockerfile.

comment.description:
Comment is an optional message that can be set when committing or
importing the image.

container.description:
Container is the ID of the container that was used to create the image.

Depending on how the image was created, this field may be empty.

Deprecated: this field is omitted in API v1.45, but kept for backward compatibility.

containerConfig.description:
ContainerConfig is an optional field containing the configuration of the
container that was last committed when creating the image.

Previous versions of Docker builder used this field to store build cache,
and it is not in active use anymore.

Deprecated: this field is omitted in API v1.45, but kept for backward compatibility.

created.description:
Created is the date and time at which the image was created, formatted in
RFC 3339 nano-seconds (time.RFC3339Nano).

This information is only available if present in the image,
and omitted otherwise.

dockerVersion.description:
DockerVersion is the version of Docker that was used to build the image.

Depending on how the image was created, this field may be empty.

graphDriver.description:
GraphDriver holds information about the storage driver used to store the
container's and image's filesystem.

metadata.description:
Metadata of the image in the local cache.

This information is local to the daemon, and not part of the image itself.

os.description: OS is the Operating System the image is built to run on.

osVersion.description:
OsVersion is the version of the Operating System the image is built to
run on (especially for Windows).

parent.description:
Parent is the ID of the parent image.

Depending on how the image was created, this field may be empty and
is only set for images that were built/created locally. This field
is empty if the image was pulled from an image registry.

repoDigests.description:
RepoDigests is a list of content-addressable digests of locally available
image manifests that the image is referenced from. Multiple manifests can
refer to the same image.

These digests are usually only available if the image was either pulled
from a registry, or if the image was pushed to a registry, which is when
the manifest is generated and its digest calculated.

repoTags.description:
RepoTags is a list of image names/tags in the local image cache that
reference this image.

Multiple image tags can refer to the same image, and this list may be
empty if no tags reference the image, in which case the image is
"untagged", in which case it can still be referenced by its ID.

rootFS.description:
RootFS contains information about the image's RootFS, including the
layer IDs.

size.description: Size is the total size of the image including all layers it is composed of.

variant.description: Variant is the CPU architecture variant (presently ARM-only).

x-enum-comments.PERM_ADMIN: System ops: restart, shutdown, update, terminal
x-enum-comments.PERM_LOGIN_WEAK: Any logged-in user, no MFA check
x-enum-comments.PERM_RESOURCES: Manage containers, storage, devices, backups, cron
x-enum-comments.PERM_RESOURCES_READ: View containers, storage, devices, backups, cron, metrics

x-enum-descriptions: System ops: restart, shutdown, update, terminal
View containers, storage, devices, backups, cron, metrics
Manage containers, storage, devices, backups, cron
Any logged-in user, no MFA check
